issues.claim: What is stolen is not throughput (that is a lesser casualty); what is stolen is discrimination
issues.objection: The verse names what is stolen as prajñā — discriminative knowledge. The engineering layer scopes prajñā to 'engineering judgment / discrimination' which is narrower than the Sanskrit's reach (Shankara: ātma-anātma-viveka). This is silent rescoping but is honest in direction — the engineering layer scopes DOWN, never UP, which is the safe direction. The source pack preserves the fuller meaning. Acceptable. Note that 'throughput' as the lesser casualty is a true downstream observation about engineer-input-stream interactions and does not contradict the verse — it just specifies what the engineering analog is and is not about.

verdict_reason: Reverse-translating the engineering layer back to the verse's Sanskrit structure: (1) un-restrained input stream → caratām indriyāṇām (wandering senses); (2) un-yoked mind drawn behind → mano 'nuvidhīyate (mind led along); (3) eroded discriminative judgment → harati prajñām (steals prajñā); (4) wind-boat metaphor → vāyur nāvam ivāmbhasi (verbatim). The compatibility is structural and verbatim. The engineering layer scopes prajñā downward (yogic-technical → engineering-judgment) but does not invert any claim. The falsifiability clause closes the misread that this licenses isolation. The counter-example correctly identifies in-task input as not-the-verse. No distortion. Pass on both 3.6 and 7.10.
